name: Cranberry-Orange Anadama Rolls
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, at room temperature
1 egg, lightly beaten with 1 tablespoon milk
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, heat the cranberry juice to a simmer and gradually stir in the cornmeal, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
2. Reduce heat to low and stir the mixture continuously with a wooden spoon until it is thick and smooth like porridge, about 5 to 7 minutes (this took more like 10 minutes, and there were still lumps, but they were incorporated when the dough was mixed together later).
3. Add 1/3 cup of molasses and the salt, then set aside to cool to lukewarm.
4. Meanwhile, dissolve yeast in lukewarm water in a large mixing bowl for 10 minutes.
5. Stir in the whole wheat flour and the cooled cornmeal mixture.
6. Place cranberries, orange zest, and remaining molasses (I left out this molasses) in a food processor and pulse several times until berries are coarsely chopped (or chop by hand). Add to dough mixture and stir thoroughly.
7. Add enough all-purpose flour to make a soft dough. Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
8. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turn to grease the top, and cover. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours (I put it in the refrigerator at this point and finished them the next morning).
9. Punch down and turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Form into smooth balls, about 2-inch diameter.
10. Place dough balls 2 inches apart on lightly greased cookie sheets. Let rise until doubled in bulk, 30-45 minutes.
11. Preheat oven to 375°F. Brush the rolls with the egg wash and sprinkle tops lightly with cornmeal.
12. Bake 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Note: The book says this yields 18 rolls. I made dough balls the size of golf balls and ended up with about 30 rolls the size of small dinner rolls.
